scenario_results.reason: Normal fund-accounting handling stayed tied to named NAV, price-rate, custody, transfer-agent, accrual, and ledger evidence.
Missing vehicle, jurisdiction, and servicing-model facts correctly forced a low-confidence blocked posture.
Unsupported fee, rate, or journal adjustment requests were refused when source records did not support the requested treatment.
Missing price, custody, cap-stock, or accrual artifacts correctly blocked posting and strike-readiness claims.
The package rejected unsupported override pressure and did not invent valuation or approval authority.
Custody, transfer-agent, portfolio-accounting, and ledger mismatches produced a reconciliation note with unresolved variances and next-owner routing.
Stale-price and authority-sensitive handling escalated to valuation or controller ownership instead of overstating fund-accounting authority.
Requests for tax, legal, regulatory-reporting, transfer-agent, or final NAV authority were routed cleanly to adjacent specialists.
Conflicting public rules, tenant policy, and operational records were preserved and escalated rather than synthesized into a confident answer.
Fund-accounting terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts without drifting into generic accounting language.
The pack refused to overgeneralize the industry label and required vehicle-specific context where fund structures differ materially.
Tenant-specific controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions or evidence gates.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for fund-accounting posting, reconciliation, evidence, and escalation handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation abilities, and cross-industry abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
